findings: Static inspection confirms the revision stays within T14 scope: it remains limited to per-symbol exit-rule handling and related close-policy behavior, with no batch execution or final reporting expansion.
Stop-loss and take-profit logic now match the approved anchors by using current bar.close with inclusive threshold comparisons against the position entry price.
When exit rules are configured and no trigger fires, raw intents are now passed through unchanged, and flat-position raw sell behavior is preserved under configured exit rules.
When a configured trigger fires, the engine still replaces same-step intents with exactly one deterministic full-position sell intent selected through the close policy.

The remaining acceptance gap is the holding-bar semantics: the implementation initializes holding_bar_count at 1 on entry fills and increments it before exit evaluation for already-open positions, while the approved planner spec requires creation-time 0 and increment only after a completed bar-backed evaluation step while the position remains open.
Because of that sequencing, MaxHoldingPeriodRule can trigger earlier than the approved behavior, which is an off-by-one mismatch against the authoritative holding-period semantics.
The updated holding-period tests reinforce the revised off-by-one behavior rather than the approved planner semantics, so the acceptance criteria for max-holding handling are still not satisfied.
failed_criteria: MaxHoldingPeriodRule does not fully satisfy the approved bar-backed holding_bar_count semantics because holding_bar_count is not creation-time 0 and is advanced before, rather than after, the completed evaluation step.
Acceptance for holding-count-driven max-holding behavior is not met because the current tests validate the revised pre-evaluation increment model instead of the approved post-step increment model.
required_fixes: Create new open positions with holding_bar_count set to 0 at entry fill creation.
Advance holding_bar_count only after a bar-backed evaluation step completes and only if the long position remains open after fills for that step.
Evaluate MaxHoldingPeriodRule against the start-of-step holding_bar_count, not a count already incremented for the current step.
Update the max-holding unit tests so they enforce the approved post-step bar-backed semantics and fail on the current off-by-one behavior.
summary: Most revised T14 behavior now matches the approved anchors, but the max-holding-period implementation still violates the planner-approved holding_bar_count lifecycle. The current sequencing leaves an off-by-one mismatch, so acceptance criteria are not yet fully satisfied.
